records.flag_question: Al Shedim (deep Ayleid/Egyptian high-level dungeon) sets MinLevel=15, MaxLevel=0. Requiem's comparable high-level lore dungeons (the Apocrypha Black Book zones DLC2Book01-07DungeonZone) carry MinLevel=25. This is pitched as a high-level quest mod, so the 15 floor sits a full tier below Requiem's elite comparable. Doctrine DEFAULT is leave-alone (the Reqtificator's openEncounterZones auto-sets DisableCombatBoundary; difficulty is carried by the fixed-level NPCs [NPC lane] + Level-1 lists, NOT the zone) — so raising the floor is an OPTIONAL tier-alignment, not a correctness fix. Raise MinLevel toward ~25 to match Requiem's elite-dungeon floor, or leave at 15? Decision belongs to Aaron / the intended tier.
records.comparable: Requiem.esp DLC2Book01-07DungeonZone (Apocrypha) MinLevel=25 MaxLevel=0 NeverResets; REQ_Whiterun_HallOfTheDeadCatacombs MinLevel=0 (mundane). Requiem uses MinLevel as a deliberate elite-tier floor.

field_checklist.difficulty_source: fixed-level NPCs (NPC lane), not the zone

records.rationale: Blinded to the existing Authoria patch; derived vs Requiem's own live Apocrypha zones. Flagged (not silently skipped) because the mod's floor is a tier below Requiem's comparable high-level dungeons — but flagged (not patched) because doctrine puts de-leveling in the NPC/list layers and explicitly calls touching ECZN level a common mistake. fields_to_set is the RECOMMENDED resolution pending decision, not an applied change (lane is read-only).

records.flag_question: Khenzedum sets MinLevel=12, MaxLevel=0, Flags=0 (note: no NeverResets, unlike the other two zones — this dungeon respawns). Lowest floor of the three, and it lacks the NeverResets flag its siblings carry. Against Requiem's comparable high-level floor (Apocrypha=25) the 12 floor is ~half a tier. Raise MinLevel toward Requiem's elite-dungeon floor (~20-25), or leave at 12? Also confirm whether the missing NeverResets flag is intentional (a respawning high-level lair). Doctrine default = leave the zone (difficulty is the NPC lane's fixed levels); optional tier-alignment only.
records.comparable: Requiem.esp Apocrypha Black Book zones MinLevel=25; mundane catacomb MinLevel=0. Sibling GF zones use NeverResets; this one does not.

field_checklist.difficulty_source: fixed-level NPCs (NPC lane), not the zone
field_checklist.recommendation: optional raise to ~20-25; default leave; confirm NeverResets intent

records.rationale: Flagged: lowest floor + the one zone without NeverResets (a real per-record outlier the no-extrapolation rule exists to catch — I did not assume the sibling zones' flags carried here). fields_to_set is a recommended value pending Aaron's tier decision; lane is read-only.

records.flag_question: Tomb of the Forgotten Draugr sets MinLevel=15, MaxLevel=0, Flags=NeverResets. A draugr tomb (dangerous undead) pitched as high-level content. Requiem's comparable elite lore-dungeon floor is 25; a generic Requiem draugr crypt (e.g. Hall of the Dead) is 0 — so the right floor depends on whether this tomb is elite (toward 25) or a mid-tier crypt (leave ~15). Doctrine default = leave the zone; raising is optional tier-alignment (difficulty carried by the tomb's fixed-level draugr, NPC lane).
records.comparable: Requiem.esp Apocrypha zones MinLevel=25 (elite) vs REQ_Whiterun_HallOfTheDeadCatacombs MinLevel=0 (mundane crypt). This tomb at 15 sits between the two.

field_checklist.difficulty_source: fixed-level draugr (NPC lane), not the zone
field_checklist.recommendation: optional raise to ~25 if elite; default leave at 15

records.rationale: Flagged: the floor is mid-band vs Requiem's comparables; correct value hinges on the tomb's intended tier, which is Aaron's call. fields_to_set is a recommended value pending decision; lane is read-only.

notes: Freshness probe PASS: houseCARL on instance E:\Skyrim Modding\ARR 2.0, profile 'Authoria - Requiem Reforged - NSFW Profile'; Requiem.esp + Requiem for the Indifferent.esp both in the override chain of 012EB7:Skyrim.esm. Authority derived from the live winner.

Enumeration matches expectations exactly: LVLI=6, LVLN=0, LVSP=0, CONT=20, ECZN=3. LVLN and LVSP confirmed 0 via cross_plugin_query defined_in=true (Gray Fox Cowl.esm defines no leveled NPC or leveled spell lists — nothing to disposition for those two types).

BLINDING honored: every Gray Fox Cowl.esm record read as the mod's OWN version. LVLI and CONT winners ARE Gray Fox Cowl.esm (override_depth=1), so winner==own. The 3 ECZN winners are 'Authoria - Patch - Gray Cowl of Nocturnal Requiem.esp' (override_depth=2) — read explicitly with plugin='Gray Fox Cowl.esm' to simulate a fresh patch; the existing patch was never read.

ALL 6 LVLI are already flat (every entry Level=1) — Requiem's de-leveling model. These are the mod's OWN creature death-item / body-part / custom-automaton loot lists (baseVersion==null, escape the Reqtificator merge) but need no hand de-levelling because there is no Level>1 gate anywhere. The confirmed field-failure this pass exists to catch (a mod's own list shipping vanilla-style level gates) did NOT occur here — Gray Fox Cowl authored flat lists.

Sublist-tree checked without extrapolation: manny_GF_LI_AncestralCheetah (parent) references manny_GF_LI_AncestralCheetahBodyParts (child); both read directly, both flat. No gate hidden one level down.
REQ_NULL preserved: manny_GF_LI_DwarvenPraetorian entry [8] references REQ_NULL_LootDwarvenArrows10 (Requiem's own intentional removal) — left intact, never stripped, never added-to, per doctrine.

ALL 20 CONT are doctrine-clean and require no re-pointing: 3 empty/cosmetic, 2 quest keys, 4 fixed unique/quest rewards (unique items owned by weapon/armor lanes), 4 merchant vendor chests, 7 furniture/loot chests. Every loot, furniture, and vendor container references Requiem-curated leveled lists (LItem*/Loot*/Vendor* families) rather than dumping raw gear — several reference Requiem-DEFINED forms directly (REQ_Potion_CureDisease in Telrav, REQ_LI_VendorBlacksmith_Minimum in Malpenix), confirming they were authored to Requiem's vendor/loot template. CONT is not in the merge, but since each container's referenced lists resolve to Requiem winners, contents inherit Requiem's balance at runtime.

ECZN is the only judgment surface in this lane. Decisive comparable read: Requiem itself sets MinLevel=25 on high-level lore dungeons (DLC2Book01-07DungeonZone / Apocrypha) and MinLevel=0 on mundane crypts (REQ_Whiterun_HallOfTheDeadCatacombs) — so Requiem uses MinLevel as a deliberate elite-tier floor, NOT something it flattens. Gray Fox Cowl's zones (MinLevel 12/15/15) sit a tier below Requiem's elite comparable. Flagged (not patched): doctrine puts de-leveling in the NPC + Level-1-list layers and explicitly calls touching ECZN level a common mistake, and the Reqtificator's openEncounterZones auto-opens every zone; so the floors are Requiem-consistent-in-direction and the raise is an OPTIONAL tier-alignment, Aaron's call. fields_to_set values are RECOMMENDED resolutions, not applied (lane is read-only).
Khenzedum ECZN is a real per-record outlier: Flags=0 (no NeverResets) while both sibling zones carry NeverResets — surfaced explicitly rather than assumed uniform.

Out-of-scope observation (not this lane's enumeration): the mod's NEW loot items (Duneripper Blood, Ancestral Cheetah parts, Desert Wolf Pelt, Praetorian Dynamo Core) reach the world via their creatures' DeathItem lists and fixed quest containers — they are creature-specific / quest-fixed drops, so they do NOT need distribution into Requiem's generic world loot lists. No 'placed-into' work is warranted for this plugin; its own lists are self-contained and flat.
